text: Hi there, my name is Dylan Sanford, a snowboard instructor at Killington, Vermont, here for
About.com.
Today we're going to learn how to load and unload from a chairlift when snowboarding.
Before getting on a chairlift, you'll want to do a few J-turns to get the feeling of
riding one-footed.
They're called J-turns because that is the feeling of the track made in the snow by your
snowboard.
Start by getting comfortable stepping in between the bindings.
You'll want to place your back foot just in front of your rear binding.
Once you can do that and not look at your feet, then you are ready to attempt the J-turn.
First, point your board down a gradual hill.
The more gradual, the better.
Stand in between the bindings, allowing your board to glide, keeping your front knee bent
more than the back knee.
Start to move your hips over the toe edge of your snowboard.
This will bring your weight to the ball of your front foot.
Then turn your shoulders up the hill so that your back is facing downhill.
As you do this a few times, you will try to move your hips over your heel edge to try
the same thing on your heel side.
This will help with offloading chairlifts as you start to ride down the mountain.
To load and unload a chairlift, take a moment to make sure that the terrain open from the
top of the chairlift is at your level of riding.
Next, skate to the load here sign.
Place your back foot off of your board next to your front binding on the toe edge side
of your board.
This will put you in a position so that all you need to do is let the chair sit you down.
To unload, you will need to stand up and not push off the chair, as in a straight glide.
Place your back foot against the inside of your back binding and ride to a stop.
Remember, it's important to stay centered and neutral.
Move quickly away from the chairlift.
You will be able to get the chairlift off of the board and ride to the next rider.
Thanks for watching.
To learn more, visit us on the web at about.com.
